hero.question: Will Tom Steyer win the California Governor Election in 2026?
hero.prob_take: At 6.1%, the market has essentially written Steyer's political obituary after his 2020 and 2022 flops, pricing in near-zero shot despite his billionaire wallet and name recognition in California. The real question is whether a fractured Democratic primary with an absent incumbent creates an opening that his money could exploit, but the odds look fair given he's burned through credibility and California voters have already rejected him twice.

daily_take.headline: Steyer's 2026 California Comeback Looks Overcooked at 6.1%
daily_take.deck: Tom Steyer's California governor odds jumped 4.2 points in 24 hours on basically no news, suggesting someone's positioning ahead of an announcement or major donor commitment. The price is generous. Steyer ran a credible 2018 gubernatorial race and has the fortune to spend at will, but California Democrats are consolidating around incumbent Newsom and rising stars like Newsom's political ecosystem. At 6.1%, you're getting a candidate who finished third last time and faces a narrower lane now. Smart money fades this pop.

sidebar.headline: Iran conflict premium evaporating as geopolitical risk reprices downward, but 22.5% still contains a real conflict event. Watch this close to 25%.
sidebar.label: US forces enter Iran by March 31: 22.5%

sidebar.headline: Arizona's tournament odds surged 2.2 points on recruiting news, but 18.6% assumes NCAA tournament success that doesn't align with Big 12 talent depth. Short this bounce.
sidebar.label: Arizona 2026 NCAA Tournament: 18.6%

sidebar.headline: Anduril IPO odds cratered 22 points in one session. This wasn't insider selling. This was market makers repricing on typical late-stage venture cycle dynamics. Recovery play if defense budget outlook strengthens.
sidebar.label: Anduril IPO before 2027: 19.0%
